title: Parker House rolls
subtitle: American butter-folded dinner rolls with a soft, pillowy crumb
excerpt: An enriched yeasted dough shaped by folding a disc of dough over itself with a butter ridge inside. The fold creates a natural pull-apart line and a soft interior. The rolls are baked close together in a dish, brushed with butter twice, and served warm. One of the great American dinner rolls.
sourceNotes: Parker House rolls were created at the Parker House Hotel in Boston, Massachusetts, reportedly in the 1870s. The defining technique is the fold of the dough disc over itself after brushing with butter, which creates the characteristic shape and the soft pull-apart interior. The recipe appears in numerous American baking compendiums from the late 19th century onward. The formula here follows the classic enriched milk dough proportions common to hotel baking of the period.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked rolls in a bag for up to 2 months. Reheat in a low oven for 8 to 10 minutes from frozen.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Shape and refrigerate overnight on the tray. Bake from the fridge, adding 5 minutes.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: conventional oven. Fan at 170°C. The rolls should be golden on top and set at the base.

glossaryTerms.definition: The shaping technique for Parker House rolls: a round disc of dough is brushed with butter down its centre line, then folded in half so the two halves overlap. The butter ridge inside softens the interior as the roll bakes and creates the pull-apart line. The fold also gives the roll its characteristic elongated, slightly domed shape.

content.text: is the whole recipe. The dough is straightforward enriched bread; the shaping is what makes it. Roll each piece to a disc, brush butter across the centre, fold in half, and press. The rolls bake together in a tin and develop a soft side crust where they touch. Brush with more butter as soon as they come out.

Combine flour, yeast, sugar, and salt. Add the warm milk, warm water, egg, and 60 g of melted butter. Mix to a soft dough and knead for 8 minutes by hand until smooth. Cover and leave to rise for 60 minutes until doubled in size.

Divide the dough into 16 equal pieces (about 58 g each). Roll each piece out to an oval about 10 × 8 cm. Brush melted butter across the centre of each oval. Fold the oval in half so the two ends almost meet but one side slightly overhangs the other. Place the folded rolls close together in a greased 30 × 20 cm tin, folded edge down. Cover loosely and prove for 30 minutes until puffed and touching.

Bake at 190°C conventional (170°C fan) for 18 to 22 minutes until the tops are gold and the rolls feel light and springy. Brush with the remaining melted butter immediately on coming out of the oven. Leave to cool in the tin for 5 minutes, then serve warm.
